translations.descricao: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is defined as abnormalities in kidney structure or function for more than three months, with implications for health. The global prevalence is estimated at 9.1% to 13.4% in adults. In Brazil, the prevalence is approximately 10.2% in the adult population. Early screening aims to identify initial stages to slow progression.
translations.sus.indicacao: Screening recommended for individuals with risk factors such as diabetes mellitus, arterial hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, age over 60 years, family history of CKD, obesity, and smoking.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults over 18 years with cardiovascular or renal risk factors; prioritize patients with diabetes and hypertension.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: KDIGO (2024) recommends assessment of eGFR and albuminuria in adults with risk factors, including diabetes, hypertension, age >60 years, family history, and cardiovascular risk markers. The Brazilian Society of Nephrology (2022) endorses similar screening for early detection.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: The global prevalence of CKD is 9.1% to 13.4% in adults, affecting about 700 million people. In Brazil, it is estimated at 10.2% of the adult population.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: The global incidence is approximately 133 cases per million inhabitants per year. In Brazil, the annual incidence is about 120-150 per million.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: CKD contributes to 4.9 million global deaths in 2021, being the 9th leading cause. In Brazil, it accounts for about 5% of deaths from non-communicable chronic diseases.
